TM = YYYYMMDDHHmm (YYYY=year, MM=month, DD=day, HH=hour, mm=minute) in UTC
ET = excel decimal time 
T = median value of temperature from centered intervals (e.g. value at 10:00 is median from 9:30-10:30)
T_std = standard deviation of temperature within the interval 
T_N = number of data points for temperature within the interval 
T_max = maximum temperature within the interval 
T_min = minimum temperature within the interval 
T_flag = quality flag 
S = median value of salinity from centered intervals (e.g. value at 10:00 is median from 9:30-10:30)
S_std = standard deviation of salinity within the interval 
S_N = number of data points for salinity within the interval 
S_max = maximum salinity within the interval 
S_min = minimum salinity within the interval 
S_flag = quality flag 
Quality flags:  -1=No flag available, 1=OK, 1-2 : includes corrected data, 4-5 : includes missing data, fraction indicates how much missing or corrected data was incorporated (low end: none, high end: all), 9=missing
